title: GitHub Issues import/export core module (API + field mapping)
description: Build the core GitHub Issues integration module. Import fetches issues from the GitHub REST API, maps label conventions to Waystation fields, and writes IssueRecords through the single write path. Export reads IssueRecords from the ledger, maps Waystation fields back to GitHub labels/state, and pushes via the API. All optional — degrades gracefully when no token is configured.
acceptance: src/core/gh.ts exports importGitHubIssues(root, repo, token) and exportGitHubIssues(root, repo, token).

Import: fetches open issues from GET /repos/{repo}/issues, maps to IssueRecord schema, writes each via createIssue() (existing write path). Skips issues already present in the ledger (dedup by id: gh-{number}). Returns CommandResult with import count + list of created ids.

Export: reads issue records from loadIssues(root), for each calls POST /repos/{repo}/issues (new) or PATCH /repos/{repo}/issues/{number} (existing). Maps Waystation status → GitHub state (open/closed), severity/type → labels.

Field mapping is a named configuration record: a mapping of GitHub labels ↔ Waystation fields. Example: label 'critical' → severity='critical', label 'bug' → type='bug'. Export reverses the mapping: severity='critical' → label 'critical'.
Token via GITHUB_TOKEN env var; if missing, returns a coded diagnostic (no_github_token). All API calls use Bun.fetch — no new dependencies.
bun:test: import creates issue records from a mock GitHub API response; export generates correct API payloads from ledger issues; missing token returns diagnostic.
typecheck, biome check, and waystation validate all clean.
notes: API: GitHub REST v3 (api.github.com). Use Bun.fetch with Authorization: Bearer token header. Rate limiting not handled in V1 — one-shot bulk operations. Field mapping: read from config section or hard-code reasonable defaults (label 'bug'/'feature'/'question' → type; label 'critical'/'high'/'medium'/'low' → severity). Export: check if issue record's id matches gh-{number} pattern to determine create vs update. Dedup import: check loadIssues() for existing gh-{number} ids.
